_comment.purpose: Custom Patch Manager baseline for Amazon Linux 2 instances.
design_notes: This baseline enforces automatic approval of Critical and Important security patches after a 7-day delay.
The 7-day delay provides a buffer to catch problematic patches before fleet-wide deployment.
Low and Medium severity patches require manual approval to prevent unnecessary churn.
Bugfix and Enhancement classifications are excluded from auto-approval — security patches only.
Rejected patches list blocks known problematic packages from being installed.
compliance_notes: Patch SLAs reflect common compliance framework requirements:
Critical: 7 days (aligns with CISA KEV remediation guidance for internet-facing systems)
Important: 14 days (aligns with NIST SP 800-40 guidance for high severity)
Adjust auto_approval_delay_days to match your organization's patching SLA policy.

baseline_configuration.Name: custom-linux-baseline
baseline_configuration.Description: Custom patch baseline for Amazon Linux 2. Auto-approves Critical and Important security patches after 7 days. Requires manual approval for Moderate and Low severity.

GlobalFilters._comment: Global filters apply before approval rules. Used to exclude patch classifications that should never be auto-approved.

PatchRules._comment: Rule 1: Auto-approve Critical security patches after 7 days.
Rule 2: Auto-approve Important security patches after 7 days.
Rule 3: Auto-approve Moderate security patches after 14 days. ComplianceLevel MEDIUM means missing these patches triggers a Medium finding.

ApprovedPatches._comment: Explicitly approved patches outside the approval rules above. Use for emergency approvals or patches that do not match the rule filters.

RejectedPatches._comment: Patches listed here will never be installed regardless of approval rules. Use for packages with known stability issues or incompatibilities in your environment.
RejectedPatches._block_vs_allow_note: BLOCK prevents installation entirely. Use ALLOW_AS_DEPENDENCY only if a rejected package is required as a transitive dependency by an approved patch.

baseline_configuration._patch_group_note: Instances must be tagged with 'Patch Group: AmazonLinux2-Standard' to use this baseline. See runbook-patching.md Step 2 for tagging instructions.

compliance_levels_reference._comment: Reference guide for ComplianceLevel values and their meaning in Patch Manager reporting.
compliance_levels_reference.CRITICAL: Missing patch generates a Critical compliance finding. Blocks compliant status immediately.
compliance_levels_reference.HIGH: Missing patch generates a High finding.
compliance_levels_reference.MEDIUM: Missing patch generates a Medium finding.
compliance_levels_reference.LOW: Missing patch generates a Low finding.
compliance_levels_reference.INFORMATIONAL: Missing patch is recorded but does not affect compliance status.
compliance_levels_reference.UNSPECIFIED: Compliance impact is determined by the patch severity metadata from the vendor.

severity_sla_policy._comment: Patch remediation SLA targets for this environment. Update to match your organization's policy.

Critical.rationale: Critical CVEs represent active exploitation risk. 7-day window balances testing buffer with exposure window.

Important.rationale: Important patches address significant vulnerabilities. 14-day SLA allows for controlled maintenance window scheduling.

Moderate.rationale: Moderate patches present limited risk. 30-day window allows batching with regular maintenance cycles.

Low.rationale: Low severity patches bundled into quarterly patching cycles. Manual approval required.

aws_cli_deployment._comment: Commands to register this baseline in AWS and set it as the default for Amazon Linux 2.
aws_cli_deployment.create_baseline: aws ssm create-patch-baseline --name custom-linux-baseline --operating-system AMAZON_LINUX_2 --cli-input-json file://patch-policies/patch-baseline.json

tuning_guide._comment: Common adjustments for different environment types.

for_production.note: Production matches default config. Do not shorten delay below 7 days without security team approval.

for_development.note: Dev environments can use 0-day delay to stay on latest patches and catch conflicts early.

for_highly_regulated.note: Regulated environments may require shorter critical SLAs and manual approval gates for audit evidence.
